Ice_Log: 1150: Entry by Commanding Officer. At 1150 visibility being good, planned an excursion into the leads of the ice pack to determine if at this time it were practical for Force Commander to carry out his order to visit Nome. Entered the field through a hard lead at 1240 and had no difficulty until 1500 when fog shut in and heavy ice was sighted ahead. Abandoned plans of attempting to reach Nome and started to retrace courses through ice and  to southward but fog shut in thick and leads were lost. Continued working to southward, at times in good leads and at other in th~~ patches of field ice. Were unable to find the lead through which the ship had entered the field and were forced to pick short leads and work constantly to the southward maneuvering ship as necessary to effect this plan. At times the ice was very thick and the leads narrow and openings had to be forced. The ice though thick was soft and rotten At 2330 came clear of the ice field.
1240: Steaming various courses through scattered field ice.
1300: Steaming on various courses at various speeds while standing through field ice throughout the watch.
1600: As before, various courses and speeds while standing through field ice. 1620 Clear of ice pack, set course at 200. 1647 Standing into ice pack on various courses and speeds.
1820: Clear of ice pack, set course at 200.
2000: Steaming as before. Steering various courses through the ice to the southward.
2330: Came clear of the ice; set course 200
